title: rosa canina var. urbica
intro: Rosa canina var. urbica, also known as the common dog rose or European dog rose, is a shrub species that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and vitamin C. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. urbica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Rosa canina var. urbica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
